content:
- `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` all passed after the new `docs/setup_sql/` guides landed. The normal test lane skipped the ignored long-lane tests as expected, so I did not run `make test-long`.
- Updated the task file to `completed` with `<passes>true</passes>` and checked every acceptance box, then ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, which updated the Ralph ledgers and sent the completion email.
- Next I am staging the full worktree, committing with the required task-finished message including test evidence, and pushing the branch.

- Staged the full worktree, committed the docs/task/Ralph updates as `task finished 02-task-write-setup-sql-documentation: document source and destination setup SQL`, and pushed the commit to `origin/master`.
- The task is complete: docs exist, task metadata is updated, Ralph switched tasks, and the repository should now be clean aside from any concurrent user changes.
- If this context ends now, the next agent can start from the new task selected by Ralph and does not need to revisit story 28 task 02.

- Read the prior Ralph progress entry, opened the list-active-tasks skill instructions, refreshed the Ralph summaries, and inspected `.ralph/current_tasks.md`, `.ralph/current_tasks_done.md`, and `.ralph/ralph-choose-task.md`.
- The refreshed active backlog has exactly two failing non-bug tasks: story 28 task 03 and story 29 task 01. No active bug tasks remain, and the active summary shows no `<blocked_by>`, `<priority>`, or `meta-task` markers for either candidate. Story 28 tasks 01 and 02 are already done in the done ledger.
- Next I should write `.ralph/current_task.txt` to the story 28 task 03 path, because it is the most logical immediate continuation after the just-finished story 28 docs task and there is no higher-precedence active bug/blocker/priority signal overriding it.

- Selected the next Ralph task and created `.ralph/current_task.txt` with the relative path `.ralph/tasks/story-28-replace-setup-sql-with-docs-and-bash-scripts/03-task-create-bash-scripts-to-generate-sql-from-yaml.md`.
- Reasoning: the refreshed active backlog contains two failing non-bug tasks, no active bugs, no visible blockers, and no visible priority tags; story 28 task 03 is the direct continuation after story 28 task 02 was just completed, so it is the most logical next task.
- If I quit now, the next agent should start directly on story 28 task 03 and then re-run Ralph selection after that task completes and the ledgers are refreshed.

- Read prior progress, loaded `.ralph/current_task.txt`, and opened the required `tdd` and `improve-code-boundaries` skill instructions.
- Confirmed the active task is story 28 task 03 at `.ralph/tasks/story-28-replace-setup-sql-with-docs-and-bash-scripts/03-task-create-bash-scripts-to-generate-sql-from-yaml.md`.
- Next I should read the task file and story context to determine whether this turn must create/adjust a plan and quit immediately or proceed into execution.

- Read the active story 28 task 03 markdown, confirmed it had no linked plan yet, and treated this as the required planning-only turn.
- Grounded the plan in the live contract from `docs/setup_sql/`, `ingest-contract`, and the runner harness/tests, then created `.ralph/tasks/story-28-replace-setup-sql-with-docs-and-bash-scripts/03-task-create-bash-scripts-to-generate-sql-from-yaml_plans/2026-04-28-generate-setup-sql-scripts-plan.md`.
- The plan is linked from the task file and ends with `NOW EXECUTE`. It chooses a boundary shape of two standalone public bash entrypoints plus one shared internal shell helper, and it requires public-interface Rust integration tests that invoke the scripts via CLI.
- Important design note for the next agent: treat `webhook.base_url` as a real HTTPS base URL from YAML (for example `https://runner.example.internal:8443`), trim only trailing slashes, and render Cockroach sinks as `webhook-${base_url}/ingest/<mapping_id>?ca_cert=...` so the output matches the harness contract.
- No production code or tests were implemented in this turn, and no validation commands were run, because the Ralph protocol required stopping immediately after writing the approved execution plan.
- Next the agent should execute the plan in red-green slices, starting with a failing Rust integration test for the Cockroach happy path, then implement the scripts and shared helper, and only after that run `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test`.

- Read the previous Ralph progress, confirmed story 28 task 03 is active, and re-opened the `tdd` plus `improve-code-boundaries` skill instructions for this execution turn.
- Prior context says the plan file for task 03 already exists and was promoted to `NOW EXECUTE`; next I am reading the task markdown and the plan so I can execute the first red-green slice from the public shell-script interface.

- Opened the task and execution plan. The plan ends in `NOW EXECUTE`, so this turn is implementation rather than planning.
- Confirmed the canonical source-of-truth contract is `docs/setup_sql/` plus runtime tests, not the stale inline task snippet. Important mismatch: the task prose still shows `/v1/ingest/...`, but the docs/runtime contract is `/ingest/<mapping_id>`.
- Next I am checking local script dependencies and existing test conventions, then I will write the first failing Rust integration test for the Cockroach happy path.

- Added the first public-interface Rust contract test at `crates/runner/tests/setup_sql_script_contract.rs` plus Cockroach fixtures under `crates/runner/tests/fixtures/setup_sql_scripts/`.
- Ran the single targeted test in RED. It failed exactly because `scripts/generate-cockroach-setup-sql.sh` does not exist yet (`os error 2`), which is the clean starting point for implementation.
- Next I am creating the shared shell helper and the Cockroach generator with only enough behavior to satisfy this first happy-path test.

- Completed the red-green slices for both entrypoints. `scripts/generate-cockroach-setup-sql.sh`, `scripts/generate-postgres-grants-sql.sh`, and `scripts/lib/setup_sql_common.sh` now exist, and the shared helper owns the CLI plus YAML-parser boundary.
- Added Rust integration coverage for both scripts, including merge/dedup behavior, dry-run, help, invalid-input errors, and explicit fallback tests proving the `python3` parser path works when `yq` is missing.
- Next I am running direct manual script invocations against the fixtures, then the required repo-wide `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` gates before updating the task metadata.
